q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: lxml.etree.parse is called with XMLParser(load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, no_network=False), enabling XXE with network I/O and local file reads

mock_guidance.target: Mock lxml.etree.parse to intercept the call on line 41
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the first argument (the BytesIO object) to inspect the raw XML string derived from params["xml"]
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a safe, minimal lxml.etree.ElementTree (e.g., parsed from '<root/>') so that subsequent code using the result does not crash
notes: The XMLParser constructor arguments (load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, no_network=False) should also be captured or asserted on to confirm the dangerous configuration.
Ensure the mock prevents any actual network requests or filesystem reads that the real parser would attempt.
